content:
First note that 7, 11, 13 are pairwise coprime and their product is 1001.  We seek positive n<2018 with

 n²≡n²≡n² (mod 7, 11, 13)

all equal to the same residue r.  Since that common r must lie in 0…6, we check which of 0,1,2,3,4,5,6 are quadratic residues mod 7, 11, 13:

– QR(7)={0,1,2,4}
– QR(11)={0,1,3,4,5,9}
– QR(13)={0,1,3,4,9,10,12}

The intersection in 0…6 is r=0,1,4.  For each r the number of solutions of n²≡r (mod 1001) is

  r=0 ⇒1⋅1⋅1=1  
  r=1 ⇒2⋅2⋅2=8  
  r=4 ⇒2⋅2⋅2=8  

so there are 1+8+8=17 residue‐classes mod 1001.  In each full block of 1001 consecutive n there are 17 solutions.  Since 2017=2·1001+15, in 1…2002 we get 2·17=34 solutions, and among 2003…2017 only the residues 1 and 2 mod 1001 (namely n=2003,2004) lie in our solution‐classes.  Hence in all 1≤n<2018 there are

  34+2=36

such n.  Answer: \boxed{36}.

  r=1 ⇒2⋅2⋅2=8

  34+2=36

such n.  Answer: \boxed{36}.
